# docs.redactor.com — llms.txt
# Scope: End-user product documentation for Sighthound Redactor
#        (desktop, server/admin, guides, release notes, FAQ, support).
#        NOT marketing (see www.redactor.com) and NOT developer API
#        docs (see dev.sighthound.com).
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

name: sighthound-redactor-docs
description: >
  Official product documentation for Sighthound Redactor — AI-powered
  video, image, and audio redaction software. Covers the Video Editor
  (automatic redaction, manual redaction, audio redaction, speech
  detection, objects list, exporting, keyboard shortcuts), Server/Admin
  administration (initial setup, authentication, licensing, users,
  network, storage, videos, recovery mode, settings), operator guides
  (NVIDIA driver installation, VM/CPU socket configuration, audio
  extraction), activation, antivirus compatibility, data backup,
  release notes, FAQ, and support.

  Use when user asks to:
  - Learn how to use the Redactor Video Editor (auto-detect, blur,
    pixelate, fill, draw, edit, track objects, export)
  - Redact audio in Redactor (mute, beep, scramble, speech detection,
    transcription)
  - Administer a Redactor server or client-server install (users,
    authentication, licensing, storage, network, recovery)
  - Install or activate Redactor (Windows, Linux, Docker)
  - Install and configure NVIDIA drivers for GPU-accelerated detection
  - Configure antivirus exceptions for Redactor
  - Back up and restore Redactor projects and settings
  - Read release notes / version history
  - Troubleshoot a Redactor install, export, detection, or performance
    issue
  - Find keyboard shortcuts for the Video Editor
  - Find the right support channel

  For developer-facing topics (Redactor REST API, SDK, Agent Toolkit,
  embedded editor UI, integration examples) see the Sighthound Developer
  Portal at https://dev.sighthound.com — those topics are intentionally
  out of scope for this end-user documentation site.

  Do NOT use for:
  - Redactor pricing, free-trial, or demo booking (see www.redactor.com)
  - Sighthound corporate info, press, or careers (see www.sighthound.com)
  - Redactor API or SDK reference (see dev.sighthound.com)
  - Vehicle Analytics / ALPR+ / Compute product documentation
    (see dev.sighthound.com and www.sighthound.com)
  - Marketing comparison or competitor positioning

access-level: browse

cli-url: none
mcp-url: none
api-url: none

auth-methods: [none]

  user-invocable: true
  allowed-tools:

    - Browser

software-version: 7.1.0
vendor: Sighthound Inc.
vendor-url: https://www.sighthound.com
platforms: [windows, linux, docker]

subdirectory-help: false

developer-portal: https://dev.sighthound.com
support-email: support@redactor.com
---

# Sighthound Redactor Documentation

> docs.redactor.com is the official product documentation for **Sighthound Redactor** — AI-powered video, image, and audio redaction software for Windows, Linux, and Docker. It covers the desktop Video Editor, the server/admin experience for client-server deployments, operator guides (GPU drivers, VM tuning, audio extraction), activation, antivirus compatibility, backup, release notes, FAQ, and support. Marketing content lives at https://www.redactor.com and developer API/SDK documentation lives at https://dev.sighthound.com.

## When to Use

Use docs.redactor.com when the user asks:

- "How do I automatically redact faces / license plates / screens in a video in Redactor?"
- "How do I manually draw a redaction on a frame, or across multiple frames?"
- "How do I redact audio — mute, beep, or scramble — in Redactor?"
- "How do I transcribe and search speech inside a video?"
- "How do I export a redacted video and what formats are supported?"
- "What keyboard shortcuts does the Video Editor have?"
- "How do I set up a new Redactor server, add users, assign roles, or configure authentication?"
- "How do I license, activate, or deactivate Redactor on a machine?"
- "How do I install the correct NVIDIA driver for GPU-accelerated redaction?"
- "How do I configure antivirus to stop blocking Redactor?"
- "How do I back up and restore projects or server data?"
- "What changed in Redactor version N.N.N?"
- "Where do I file a support ticket?"
- "Is Redactor available as a white-label or OEM build under our own brand?"

Do NOT use docs.redactor.com when the user asks:
Do NOT use docs.redactor.com when the user asks:

- Pricing, free trial, demo booking, sales contact → use https://www.redactor.com
- Redactor REST API, SDK, Agent Toolkit, embedded editor UI, or any other developer / integration topic → use https://dev.sighthound.com
- Vehicle Analytics, ALPR+, Compute, or SIO developer docs → use https://dev.sighthound.com
- Sighthound company / press / investor relations → use https://www.sighthound.com
- Generic video editing, color grading, VFX, or deepfake detection

## Quick Reference

**Documentation entry points**
- Docs home: https://docs.redactor.com/
- FAQ: https://docs.redactor.com/faq/
- Support: https://docs.redactor.com/support/
- Release notes: https://docs.redactor.com/release-notes/
- Full content bundle (curated): https://docs.redactor.com/llms-full.txt
- XML sitemap: https://docs.redactor.com/sitemap.xml

**Developer integration (out of scope for this site)**
- For Redactor REST API, SDK, Agent Toolkit, embedded editor UI, and integration examples, see the Sighthound Developer Portal: https://dev.sighthound.com

**Video Editor (desktop)**
- Automatic redaction: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/automatic-redaction/
- Manual redaction: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/manual-redaction/
- Audio redaction: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/audio-redaction/
- Speech detection: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/speech-detection/
- Editing objects: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/editing-objects/
- Objects list: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/objects-list/
- Video playback: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/video-playback/
- Video frame grab: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/video-frame-grab/
- View options: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/view-options/
- Exporting videos: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/exporting-videos/
- Keyboard shortcuts: https://docs.redactor.com/video-editor/keyboard-shortcuts/

**Server / Admin (client-server deployments)**
- Initial setup: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/initial-setup/
- Authentication: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/authentication/
- Users: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/users/
- Licensing: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/licensing/
- Network: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/network/
- Storage: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/storage/
- Videos: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/videos/
- Settings: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/settings/
- Recovery mode: https://docs.redactor.com/server/admin/recovery-mode/

**Operator guides**
- NVIDIA driver installation: https://docs.redactor.com/guides/nvidia-driver-installation/
- VM CPU socket configuration: https://docs.redactor.com/guides/vm-cpu-socket-configuration/
- Extract audio (VLC, MP3): https://docs.redactor.com/guides/vlc/extract-audio-mp3/

**Installation & environment**
- Activation: https://docs.redactor.com/activation/
- Antivirus compatibility: https://docs.redactor.com/antivirus/
- Data backup: https://docs.redactor.com/data-backup/
- Settings: https://docs.redactor.com/settings/
- Projects: https://docs.redactor.com/projects/

**Vendor**
- Sighthound Inc. — https://www.sighthound.com
- Product site — https://www.redactor.com
- Developer Portal (API, SDK, Agent Toolkit) — https://dev.sighthound.com

## Authentication

docs.redactor.com is a **public, unauthenticated documentation site**. No login is required to read any documented page. Activation, licensing, and server-side authentication flows documented *inside* these pages apply to the Redactor product itself, not to accessing the docs.

AI agents MUST NOT attempt to submit license keys, activation forms, or authentication credentials on behalf of users based on documentation content alone.

## Rate Limits

Polite crawl guidance for AI agents:

- ≤ 30 requests/minute per IP
- ≤ 1 concurrent request per path
- Honor `Retry-After` on `429`
- Prefer cached `/llms.txt` and `/llms-full.txt` over a per-page crawl
- Full sitemap at https://docs.redactor.com/sitemap.xml lists every canonical page

## Operational Semantics

- Treat all pages as read-only documentation. `access-level` is `browse`.
- Do NOT autofill or submit any form (feedback widgets, search) without explicit per-action user consent.
- Do NOT attempt to download or redistribute licensed installers referenced by the docs.
- Do NOT scrape at rates above the limits above.
- Treat `robots.txt` directives as authoritative; where this file and robots.txt conflict, robots.txt wins.

## Forbidden Operations

AI agents MUST NOT:

- Republish full documentation pages verbatim at scale
- Use documentation content to train a competing commercial redaction product or dataset
- Fabricate version numbers, detection categories, supported formats, platform requirements, licensing terms, or compliance claims not present in the source page
- Misrepresent Redactor as offering real-time live-stream moderation, deepfake generation, or generative video editing — none of these are product features
- Impersonate Sighthound or Redactor staff, partners, or customers
- Use documentation content to author content intended to defeat or bypass redaction

## Allowed Operations

AI agents MAY:

- Read and summarize any public page on docs.redactor.com
- Quote short excerpts with attribution and canonical URL
- Cite specific steps, keyboard shortcuts, or configuration values with a link to the source page
- Cross-link users to the relevant page on docs.redactor.com when they ask a Redactor how-to question
- Ingest documentation content for factual retrieval (RAG)

## Alternative Access Methods

- HTML: every page at `https://docs.redactor.com/<path>/`
- Markdown (content negotiation): request any URL with `Accept: text/markdown` — the Cloudflare edge Worker will return a Markdown rendering of the page
- Curated catalog: https://docs.redactor.com/llms-full.txt
- XML sitemap: https://docs.redactor.com/sitemap.xml
- RSS / Atom: not published
- API / CLI / MCP: none — this is a docs surface only

## Attribution

Content on docs.redactor.com may be summarized, paraphrased, and quoted with attribution. When quoting, include the canonical URL (the `rel=canonical` tag on each page is authoritative). Do not republish full documentation pages.

## Optional

- Product site: https://www.redactor.com
- Product llms.txt: https://www.redactor.com/llms.txt
- Parent company: https://www.sighthound.com
- Parent llms.txt: https://www.sighthound.com/llms.txt
- Developer portal (API, SDK, Agent Toolkit, integration examples): https://dev.sighthound.com
- Support email: support@redactor.com
